exercises.name: Balance Board Single Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.alternateName: Balance Board Single Leg Hip Thrust
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your foot is centered into the middle of the balance board.

2) From there, engage your glutes and your abdominals to lift your hips up and ensure that your hips don’t rock back and forth.

3) Lie on your back with one foot flat on center of a balance board, and your free leg extended up.

4) Keep your abs engaged and your leg extended as you push your hips up, lifting from your glutes. Hold briefly. 

5) Slowly lower your hips to the start position.
 
6) When you complete one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Balance Board Single Leg Knee Drive
exercises.alternateName: Runner Single Leg Stand on Wobble Board
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your knee stays in line with your second toe and your hip. 

2. Place one foot centered on top of the balance board with the other foot off the board on the ground.

3.Stand on one leg by loading your bodyweight over top of the foot on the balance board by squeezing your glutes, leaning forward, and extending through the front leg until you are standing tall on one leg. 

4. Bring your free leg's knee up in front of you, and counter check your torso's rotation by bringing the opposite arm (opposite from your free leg) across your body. Keep your abdominals drawn in, glutes active, and active leg to minimize wobbles and maintain balance. 

5.Begin your squat by hinging your hips and sitting back, knee bending with a slight lateral press outward, and allowing the free leg to articulate backward (keeping your knee at 90 degrees).

6.Return to the standing position by squeezing your glutes, drawing abdominals in, and extending through the knee. 

7.Repeat for the prescribed number of reps."

exercises.name: Balance Board Single Leg Standing
exercises.description:
1) Starting Position Slowly and cautiously step onto the Balance Board with one foot, holding on to a wall or sturdy object for balance if necessary. 

2) Take your time adjusting your balance until you can stand upright with your other foot lifted off the floor, abs engaged, and arms extended at your sides. 

3) Action Breathe steadily as you balance in this standing position for 15-30 seconds. 

4) Gradually work your way up to holding this exercise for 1-3 minutes for an isometric core challenge. 

5) Switch sides.

exercises.name: Balance Board Single Leg Standing Hold
exercises.alternateName: Balance Board single leg standing balance with isometric static balance
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain a slightly bent knee and a slightly bent hip throughout the exercise, that’s keep your leg on and active. You’ll also want to maintain an active core.

2.Place one foot centered on top of the balance board with the other foot off the board and on the ground.

3.Stand on one leg by loading your bodyweight over top of the foot on the balance board by squeezing your glutes, leaning forward, and extending through the front leg until you are standing tall on one leg. 

4.Bring your free leg's knee up in front of you, and counter checks your torso's rotation by bringing the opposite arm (opposite from your free leg) across your body. Keep your abdominals drawn in, glutes active, and an active leg to minimize wobbles and maintain balance. 

5.Hold for the prescribed duration.

6.Once you’ve completed one side, repeat with the other foot to finish the set.

exercises.name: Balance Board Single Leg Standing with Knee Raise
exercises.description:
1) Starting Position Slowly and cautiously step onto the Balance Board with one foot, holding on to a wall or sturdy object for balance if necessary. 

2) Take your time adjusting your balance until you can stand upright with your other leg foot lifted off the floor, abs engaged, and arms extended at your sides. 

3) From there, slowly lift your knee up in front of you until the thigh is approximately parallel to the floor and the knee is bent at about 90 degrees. 

4) Gradually return to the start position and repeat.

5) Switch sides.

exercises.name: Balance Board Split Squat
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, you’ll want to try to maintain your body weight over top of the back leg as best you can during your lunge. You’ll do so by keeping your abdominals on and glutes active.

2.Place one foot centered on top of the balance board with the other foot off the board and on the ground. 
3.Squat into a lunge position by keeping your body tall over your back hip and knee. 

4.Lower to the point where your knees, hip, and ankle make 90-degree angles, and your knees are in line with your hips and second toes. 

5.Return to a split stand by squeezing your glutes, and extending through both legs to straighten your knees. 

6.Repeat for the prescribed number of reps.

7.Once you’ve completed one side, finish it with the other.

exercises.name: Balance Board Squat
exercises.description:
1.Step one foot at a time onto the balance board with your feet placed equal distances apart. 

2.Shift your bodyweight so you are centered with approximately half your bodyweight over each foot. The edges of the balance board should not touch the ground throughout. 

3.Lower yourself into a squat, keeping knees in line with your second toe and hip (lightly pressed laterally/outward/away from your body), and a neutral or a slight spinal backward bend (extension). 

4.Stand by extending through your knees, squeezing your glutes, and drawing abdominals in. 

5.Repeat prescribed number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Balance Board Standing Hold
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain a light knee bend and a light bend at the hips to maintain an active position.

2. Step one foot at a time onto the balance board with your feet placed equal distances apart. 

3. Shift your bodyweight so you are centered with approximately half your bodyweight over each foot. The edges of the balance board should not touch the ground throughout this exercise. 

4.Slightly bend your knees, keeping them in line with your second toe and hip. Extend your arms in front or to the side for additional balance. The goal is to have minimal wobbles or body movement as you do this.

5.Hold for the prescribed amount of time.

exercises.name: Band Alternating Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1) Stand with both feet on resistance band holding handles long next to your sides 

2) Slowly raise one hand up towards your shoulders, squeezing the biceps and keeping your elbows next to your sides. 

3) Slowly lower that arm back down towards the start position, then repeat on the opposite arm.

exercises.name: Band Alternating Front Raise
exercises.description: Hold the band with both hands and alternate raising one arm to the front and the other to the side, keeping the movements controlled and engaging the shoulders.

exercises.name: Band Alternating Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1) Stand with both feet on the centre of the band, hip-width apart.

2) Bring handles of the band above shoulders so that elbows are bent 90 degrees.

3) Press one arm straight up until the elbow is locked. Slowly return to the start position and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Alternating Tension Lateral Raise
exercises.description:
Stand on a resistance band and hold the handles with both hands. 

Raise your arms straight in front of you to shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in the elbows, then lower back down slowly.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Alternating Bent Over Row
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at above head height, and standing facing it.

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your arms fully extended in front of you.

3. Pull one of the handles towards your armpit, while keeping an "open chest" posture. 

4. Return to the start position under control before pulling the opposite hand in towards your armpit.

5. Continue alternating left and right for desired repetitions.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Alternating Decline Chest Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position. Slightly lean your torso forward.

3. With one foot forward, and one back, press one of your hands outwards and downwards until your arm is fully extended.

4. Return to the start position under control before pressing with the opposite hand.

5. Continue alternating left and right for the desired number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Alternating Front Raise
exercises.description: Step on the band with both feet. Alternately raise each arm forward to shoulder height. Lower with control.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Alternating Incline Curl
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands down by your sides.

3. Lean your torso slightly forward, and bring one of your hands up to chest height by bending at the elbow.

4. Slowly return to the start position, then curl with the opposite arm. 

5. Continue alternating hands for the desired repetitions.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Alternating Reverse Lunge Row
exercises.description: Anchor the band at chest height. Step back into a lunge while pulling the band toward your chest. Alternate sides.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Alternating Standing Incline Chest Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position. 

3. With one foot forward, and one back, press one of your hands outwards and upwards until your arm is fully extended.

4. Return to the start position under control before pressing with the opposite hand.

5. Continue alternating left and right for the desired repetitions.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Chest Fly
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Grab both the handles in your hands while facing away from the anchor point. 

3. Stand so that your body forms a "T" with arms parallel to the floor. 


4. With straight arms, drive your hands forwards and away from the anchor point, bringing your hands together. 
 
4. Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Chest Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position. 

3. With one foot forward, and one back, press one of your hands outwards until your arm is fully extended.

4. Return to the start position under control before pressing with the opposite hand.

5. Continue alternating left and right for the desired number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Decline Chest Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position. Slightly lean your torso forward.

3. With one foot forward, and one back, press your hands outwards and downwards until your arms are fully extended.

4. Return to the start position under control. Repeat.

exercises.name: Band Anchored High-Low Woodchop
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing the band to an anchor point at head height or above.

2. Grabbing the band with both hands, and body parallel to the band, bring the band downwards and across your body until it is down by your side. 

3.Return to starting position and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Incline Curl
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands down by your sides.

3. Lean your torso slightly forward, and bring your hands up to chest height by bending at the elbows.

4. Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Kneeling Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1) In a kneeling position, place the bands near your calves. 

2) Grab the handlebars with both hands. Your elbows should be bent. 

3) Contract your triceps and hold this position for a second.

4) Repeat for the number of reps in your set.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Low-High Woodchop
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height.

2. Stand with your chest parallel to the band, holding it in both hands, with feet shoulder-width apart. 

3. With arms extended, rotate your torso until you are facing away from the anchor point.

4. Return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Grasp the band in both hands and bring your arms up overhead, so your elbows are near your ears. 

3. Bend at the elbows, bringing your hands toward your upper back.

4. Extend your hands up towards the ceiling, while keeping your elbows in place. 

5. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat

exercises.name: Band Anchored Pistol Squat to Row
exercises.alternateName: single leg squat to row with band
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. While holding the band in both hands, do a pistol squat by sitting back into your hips, knees, and ankles on a single leg.

3. Return to the start position, then pull the handles towards your armpits, while keeping an "open chest" position. 

4. Repeat the pistol squat, and the row, until you've completed all the repetitions. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Reverse Fly
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Grab both the handles in your hands while facing the anchor point. 

3. With straight arms, drive your hands backwards and away from the anchor point so that your body forms a "T" with arms parallel to the floor. 

4. Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Side Lunge
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Grasp the handle in both hands, with your torso parallel to the band. 

3. With feet together, step outwards away from the anchor point. 

4. Bend the knee of the moving leg, reaching your hips back and descending. 

5. Push yourself back up to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Single Arm Chest Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position. 

3. With one foot forward, and one back, press one of your hands outwards until your arm is fully extended.

4. Return to the start position under control. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Single Arm Front Raise
exercises.description: Step on one end of the band. Lift the handle with one arm in front to shoulder height. Lower slowly.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Single Arm Incline Curl
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands down by your sides.

3. Lean your torso slightly forward, and bring one of your hands up to chest height by bending at the elbow.

4. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Single Arm Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Grasp the band in one hand and bring your arm up overhead, so your elbow is near your ears. 

3. Bend at the elbow, bringing your hand toward your upper back.

4. Extend your hand up towards the ceiling, while keeping your elbow in place. 

5. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Single Arm Row
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height, and standing facing it.

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your arms fully extended in front of you.

3. Pull one of the handles towards your armpit, while keeping an "open chest" posture. 

4. Return to the start position under control. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Single Arm Standing Incline Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position. 

3. With one foot forward, and one back, press one of your hands outwards and upwards until your arm is fully extended.

4. Return to the start position under control. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Single Arm Tricep Kickback
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Take the handles in both hands by your sides. 

3. Hinge at the hips, so your torso comes forward to near parallel with the floor. 

4. While keeping your elbows by your sides, drive one of your hands backwards and away from the anchor point until your arm is fully straightened. 

5. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Single Arm Tricep Pushdown
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height. 

2. Grasp the handle in both hands, keeping your elbows by your sides. 

3. Extend one of your hands down towards the floor, while keeping your elbows in place. 

4. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Squat to Row
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. While holding the band in both hands, do a squat by sitting back into your hips, knees, and ankles.

3. Return to the start position, then pull the handles towards your armpits, while keeping an "open chest" position. 

4. Repeat the squat, and the row, until you've completed all the repetitions.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Standing Incline Chest Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position. 

3. With one foot forward, and one back, press your hands outwards and upwards until your arms are fully extended.

4. Return to the start position under control.
